title: She survived a brain tumor. Now she’s running the Boston Marathon.
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Tracy Haisley, 50, from Columbia, SC, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon after surviving a brain tumor and surgery.
Haisley underwent brain surgery for a tumor and rehab before achieving her dream to run in the Boston Marathon.
Passionate about libraries, Haisley joins the Boston Public Library team to advocate for their importance and funding.

title: What Jaylen Brown said of Jayson Tatum’s return to Celtics
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jayson Tatum returned to the Celtics after a 10-month absence, scoring 15 points in a 120-100 win over the Mavericks.
Jaylen Brown praised Tatum's return, noting it boosts the Celtics' title contention and commends Tatum's commitment to the team.
Brown and Tatum maintain a strong partnership, understanding the importance of team success and playoff aspirations despite role changes.

title: Cooper Flagg was asked if he wants to be a Celtic one day. Here’s what the Maine native said.
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Cooper Flagg, a Maine native and Mavericks player, expressed his commitment to the team, despite playing at TD Garden.
Selected as the No. 1 pick, Flagg averages 20.3 points and 6.5 rebounds, highlighting his strong rookie season with Dallas.
Flagg praised the energy at TD Garden, reflecting on his childhood support for the Celtics while enjoying his first game there.

title: With Jayson Tatum back in the fold, these Celtics know their story is just beginning
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jayson Tatum returned to the Celtics lineup after 42 weeks, debuting against the Dallas Mavericks at TD Garden.
Tatum scored 15 points, 12 rebounds, and 7 assists in his first game back, missing initial shots but improving in the second half.
Celtics coach Joe Mazzulla praised Tatum's effort despite shooting struggles, marking the start of Tatum's reintegration before playoffs.

title: Jayson Tatum’s return was essentially perfect: 7 takeaways as Celtics blow out Mavericks
author: Tom Westerholm
bullets: Jayson Tatum returned to the Celtics with no minutes restriction after a 10-month injury recovery, scoring 15 points.
Neemias Queta supported Tatum's return with 16 points and 15 rebounds, showing strong performance alongside new teammate dynamics.
Nikola Vucevic will miss a month after fracturing his finger, while Jaylen Brown led the Celtics with 24 points.

title: Why Bruins made right call to stand pat at trade deadline
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bruins GM Don Sweeney decided against major trades at the deadline, focusing on long-term rebuilding and maintaining future assets.
Boston made minor AHL trades, acquiring Lukas Riechel, Alexis Gendron, and Massimo Rizzo, while trading Brett Harrison and Jackson Edward.
Sweeney emphasized commitment to future growth, retaining four first-round picks over two years, and avoiding high trade prices in a seller's market.

title: Patriots reportedly miss out on Maxx Crosby trade
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Maxx Crosby is set to be traded from the Las Vegas Raiders to the Baltimore Ravens for two first-round picks.
Crosby had interest in joining the New England Patriots but the team couldn't match Baltimore's trade offer.
The Patriots reportedly offered a trade to the Eagles for A.J. Brown and have $60 million in cap space for moves.

title: Report: Celtics center Nikola Vucevic to miss one month with broken finger
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Celtics center Nikola Vucevic fractured his right ring finger against Dallas Mavericks and is expected to miss a month.
Vucevic, acquired at the trade deadline, has averaged 11.4 points and 8.8 rebounds for the Celtics this season.
Luka Garza and Neemias Queta are expected to fill in for Vucevic during his absence, straining Celtics' front-court depth.

title: Watch: Jayson Tatum receives thunderous ovation in first game back with Celtics
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jayson Tatum returned to the Celtics' lineup against the Mavericks after recovering from a ruptured Achilles tendon.
Tatum was introduced last and received a thunderous ovation at TD Garden, marking his return to NBA action.
Tatum contributed with a put-back dunk and a step-back 3-pointer, despite missing 62 games this season.

title: Report: Patriots to trade C Garrett Bradbury to Bears
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Patriots to trade center Garrett Bradbury to Bears for a fifth-round draft pick, reported by ESPN's Adam Schefter.
The trade would become official after the new league year starts, following the retirement of Bears' center Drew Dalman.
Bradbury started every game in 2025, helping Patriots reach Super Bowl LX and significantly improve their offensive line.
